messages.subject: [RsR] boxplot: whiskers
messages.body_plain:
Hello

 

How are the lower/upper whiskers defined in the default version of
boxplot()?

Are they a possible estimation of a 95 % confidence interval?

 

Yours

 

Daniel Steiner

Langenthal


	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [RsR] boxplot: whiskers
messages.body_plain:
Hello,

>From "help(boxplot)": the argument "range", that equals 1.5 by default,
"determines how far the plot whiskers extend out from the box.  If
'range' is positive, the whiskers extend to the most extreme data point
which is no more than 'range' times the interquartile range from the box
(...)"

As far as I can see, this is cannot be easily connected to a confidence
interval for the location parameter.

Matias

Daniel Steiner wrote:
> Hello
> 
>

> boxplot()?
